description: A walking trail in Grass Valley, reached from 1000 Cooley Drive.

A Bear River site on the Colfax Highway near Grass Valley, named for the 1908 narrow-gauge railroad bridge that once crossed here — the highest railway bridge in California in its day, demolished in 1963 for the Rollins Dam project.
A network of trails off Champion Mine Road in Nevada City; their routes aren't drawn on this map yet.
A stretch of the South Yuba River known as the Emerald Pools, reached from Forest Route 18 near Nevada City.
A self-guided fall-colors tour of Nevada City starting from Broad Street; the Nevada City Chamber publishes the route.
Nevada City's Pioneer Park, on Nimrod Street — a recurring MUSE stop, from the annual Calendar of Events to 'A Filmmaking Tradition' and 'Lucky Little Towns.'
Scotts Flat Lake and its trails, off Scotts Flat Road near Nevada City; the Scotts Flat Trail's route is drawn on this map.
A self-guided walking tour of Grass Valley, beginning at Neal and South Church Streets and taking in the Del Oro Theatre, the Holbrooke Hotel, and the Mill Street historic district.
A walking trail in Alta Sierra; its route is drawn on this map.
